summary: Stellar second set with The Wheel, Not Fade Away & vibrant jams
blurb: The show shines in a high‑energy second set featuring The Wheel, Not Fade Away and Morning Dew. Brent’s organ on Not Fade Away and the tight interplay of Mickey & Bill drive the performance. Overall a solid, well‑balanced soundboard capture makes this a must‑listen for ’82 fans.
review:
On September 12, 1982 at the Lakeland Civic Center the Dead delivered a show that has aged into one of the era’s highlights. The first set rolls along with strong renditions of “Brown Eyed Women,” “Might As Well” and a punchy “West L.A. Fadeaway.” It’s the second set where the magic truly unfolds – a seamless segue from “Playing In The Band” through a crisp drum‑and‑space excursion into a soaring “The Wheel,” followed by a rousing “Not Fade Away” that showcases Brent Mydland’s adventurous B‑3 organ work, and an emotionally charged “Morning Dew.” The band’s chemistry is evident throughout; Jerry’s vocals are expressive (if occasionally weary) and his guitar cuts through with fire, while Phil’s bass, though noted as thin in the mix, still anchors the groove. Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann provide a jazzy, responsive rhythmic foundation that propels each transition. The soundboard recordings capture this energy with crisp clarity – especially the “gd82-09-12.sbd.ladner.7826.sbeok.shnf” tape, whose balanced mix highlights the vocals and instrumental interplay without distraction.

Fans will appreciate the bright natural reverb of the Sennheiser‑mic captures that lend a unique ambience while preserving detail. Whether you’re after the tight “Playing In The Band” groove, the uplifting “Sugar Magnolia,” or the heartfelt close with “Don’t Ease Me In,” this night stands out as a quintessential 1982 Dead experience.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are marked as positive, with multiple reviewers praising the second‑set flow, standout songs (The Wheel, Not Fade Away, Morning Dew) and band interplay. No negative remarks appear across analyses, just consistent acclaim for vocals, organ work and rhythmic drive, warranting a strong positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Crisp, well‑balanced soundboard mix that captures strong vocals and Brent’s organ highlight on Not Fade Away; includes the full show with excellent clarity.

key_highlights: High‑energy second set featuring The Wheel, Not Fade Away and Morning Dew.
Brent Mydland’s adventurous B‑3 organ work shines on Not Fade Away.
Tight drum‑and‑space interplay from Mickey & Bill drives the jams.
Heavy natural reverb recordings add a unique bright character without sacrificing detail.

band_performance.Jerry: Vocals are strong and expressive, though occasionally weary; guitar tone is praised and his fire on tracks like Might As Well stands out.
band_performance.Phil: Bass feels thin and low‑end under‑represented in the mix, lacking its usual fat presence.
band_performance.Bob: Singing is spot‑on with inspired phrasing; rhythm work solid even when occasionally buried in the mix.
band_performance.Brent: Adventurous B‑3 organ on Not Fade Away highlighted; keyboards sit high in the mix with strong electric‑piano licks, occasionally overly loud but overall excellent.
band_performance.Mickey: Responsive, jazzy interplay that drives the band throughout, especially during the second‑set transitions.
band_performance.Billy: Alongside Mickey, provides driving, dynamic drumming that propels the jams and supports the seamless segues.
